description:
You are a database-reliability engineer onboarding a new backup system.  
    Part of the onboarding checklist is to register a “reference moment” that all new backup windows will be scheduled from: **the first second of 1 January 2024 in UTC**.  
    Management wants this reference moment stored in a plain-text log so that automated tooling can read it later.

    Your job (one single terminal command is enough) is to:

    1. Make sure the directory **/home/user/backup_logs** exists.
    2. Create, or overwrite if it already exists, the file  
       **/home/user/backup_logs/q1-2024-start.log**
    3. Write exactly one line, terminated by the usual single newline character (**\n**), containing the text below (no additional spaces, tabs, or characters):  
       2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC

    Output format that the automated test will check:
    • The file must be located at /home/user/backup_logs/q1-2024-start.log  
    • The file must contain exactly the 24-character string “2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC” followed by a newline.  
      (In other words, its byte sequence must be:  
       32 bytes total – 24 visible characters plus the single newline “\n”, i.e. hex 0A, and no other bytes.)  
    • No other files should be modified or created.

    Hint: a single echo(1) or printf(1) invocation with the correct text, combined with shell redirection, is sufficient.
truth:
Expected file path: /home/user/backup_logs/q1-2024-start.log

    Expected content (including terminating newline, shown here with ␊ representing the newline character):

    2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC␊
name: task_000000_fc9e0b3b
